issues.name: Timing chain tensioner bleed-down
keywords: rattle for a few seconds after start
issues.mechanism: Rear hydraulic tensioner loses oil pressure overnight, allowing temporary timing chain slack on cold start before oil pressure builds.
symptom_notes: Usually strongest on cold start after sitting overnight
Often lasts a few seconds, then fades
Can be mistaken for general top-end noise
May worsen over time if guides also wear
common_misreads: Not every startup tick is lifter noise
Can be confused with exhaust manifold leak on cold start
Can be confused with accessory rattle
first_checks: Confirm whether the noise is strongest after overnight parking
Check oil level, oil condition, and service history
Listen near timing cover area during cold start
Check if the rattle fades quickly as oil pressure stabilizes

issues.name: Cam phaser wear / phaser knock
issues.mechanism: Cam phaser wear or oil-control instability causes variable valve timing noise and unstable timing behavior, especially with poor oil condition or low oil pressure control.
symptom_notes: Can be louder warm at idle than true cold-start tensioner noise
May come with drivability complaints or timing-related faults
Can sound deeper and more persistent than a brief startup rattle
common_misreads: Often blamed on generic lifter noise
Can be confused with timing chain problems even when phasers are central
first_checks: Check whether the noise continues after warm idle
Review oil viscosity and service interval history
Look for timing correlation faults if scanner data is available
Distinguish short startup rattle from persistent warm-idle knock

issues.name: AFM lifter collapse
issues.mechanism: Active Fuel Management lifter fails mechanically or hydraulically, causing valve motion loss, misfire, and upper valvetrain ticking or tapping.
symptom_notes: Often combines ticking noise with rough idle or cylinder-specific misfire
May be worse at idle and lower RPM
Can progress from intermittent misfire to clear mechanical noise
common_misreads: Can be mistaken for injector tick
Can be misdiagnosed as only a coil or spark plug problem
May be blamed on generic top-end noise until misfire becomes obvious
first_checks: Check for cylinder-specific misfire pattern
Listen for top-end mechanical tick that matches the misfire case
Do not assume ignition parts are the root cause if the tick is mechanical
Review whether the problem worsened over time from intermittent to constant
do_not_confuse_with: Simple ignition misfire without mechanical noise

issues.name: AFM oil-control instability
issues.mechanism: Oil-control problems in the AFM system reduce stable lifter operation and can create intermittent noise, unstable cylinder deactivation behavior, or misleading drivability complaints.
symptom_notes: May not begin as a full lifter collapse case
Can feel intermittent before becoming a harder failure
Oil quality and pressure behavior matter heavily
common_misreads: Can be mistaken for random ignition weakness
Can be confused with a temporary oil-pressure quirk
first_checks: Check oil level, oil condition, and maintenance history
Check whether the issue is intermittent or constant
Compare cold behavior versus warm behavior
Separate soft AFM instability from a hard collapsed-lifter failure

issues.name: Timing chain stretch
issues.mechanism: Timing chain elongation and guide wear create unstable chain control, mechanical rattle, and possible timing correlation faults if the wear progresses.
symptom_notes: Often comes from the front engine area
May appear as metallic rattle before severe drivability issues
Can coexist with timing faults or rough running if advanced
common_misreads: Can be confused with accessory noise
Can be mistaken for a brief harmless startup rattle when it is becoming a real chain problem
first_checks: Identify whether the noise comes from the timing side of the engine
Check if the rattle is brief, repeated, or becoming persistent
Check for timing-related fault codes if available
Do not dismiss metallic chain-like rattle on this engine family

issues.name: Timing guide wear progression
keywords: noise after startup and while idling
issues.mechanism: Timing guide wear reduces chain stability further, allowing longer-duration rattle, harsher chain movement, and increasing risk of major timing failure if ignored.
symptom_notes: More persistent than a very short startup event
Risk becomes more serious if noise grows louder or more frequent
common_misreads: Can be dismissed as normal BMW valvetrain sound
Can be mistaken for harmless startup noise
first_checks: Ask whether the noise is getting worse over weeks or months
Check whether the sound lasts beyond initial startup
Treat persistent metallic chain noise as higher risk
